description: Painfully, the bird was originally reported by Steve Barlow on 5/3/23, while Curt and I were birding SE CO, but we made an evening visit to Long Lake once we had returned.  About 50 mins in, we found a Black-throated Gray Warbler (which Steve also had on 5/3) and I said to Curt...if that thing is still here then the Lucy's might be too!.  Just as soon as I said that, I spotted a smaller, very pale gray warbler in the buckthorn(?) thicket on the side of the trail.  I am familiar with the species and knew when I saw zero yellows or greens on it that it was the Lucy's.  It was at first forraging on the top/edges of the low bramble with a pair of gnatcatchers and then sometimes into medium-sized "emergent" willows nearby.  We were able to watch it as it fed, sometimes hover-gleaning small insects.  It was in one of these hovers that I was able to get a photo showing the deep-rosy band on the rump.  No reddish crown patch was noticed in our observation nor were any vocalizations.  In the end, I was able to notify my friend Rob Raker who was nearby and he saw and photographed the bird as well.
